label: GGW — Senegal Western Anchor
sub: 60,000+ ha restored under ANGMV. Model agroforestry with millet, groundnut, and Acacia. Supports 2M+ people.

label: Niger — Farmer-Managed Natural Regeneration
sub: 5M+ ha regenerated since 1980s via FMNR. Largest verified dryland restoration globally. Reduced famine risk for 2.5M farmers.

label: Ethiopia — Green Legacy (GGW Eastern Anchor)
sub: 20B+ seedlings planted 2019–2024 under PM Abiy Ahmed's Green Legacy initiative. Survival rates disputed (est. 50–80%).

label: Mali — Sahel Degradation Front
sub: GGW corridor zone. Combined conflict and climate pressures have hindered restoration progress. ~2M ha at risk of further degradation.

label: Burkina Faso — Zaï Restoration
sub: Traditional Zaï pit planting technique has restored 200,000+ ha of degraded Sahel farmland. FAO-recognized best practice for dryland restoration.

label: China — Three-North Shelterbelt ('Green Wall of China')
sub: World's largest tree planting program (1978–present). 66M ha planted across semi-arid north China. Criticized for monoculture approach reducing biodiversity.

label: Pakistan — Billion Tree Tsunami (KPK)
sub: 1.06B trees planted 2014–2017, IUCN-verified. Expanded nationally as Ten Billion Tree Tsunami (2018–2023). Focus on native conifers and mixed species.

label: India — Rajasthan Aravalli Reforestation
sub: Part of India's NDC target to create 2.5–3 B tonnes additional carbon sink by 2030. Aravalli range afforestation aims to restore semi-arid corridors.

label: Amazon — Pará Deforestation Frontier
sub: Pará state leads Brazilian Amazon deforestation. 'Arc of Deforestation' driven by cattle ranching and soy farming. Amazon Fund re-activated 2023.

label: Brazil — Atlantic Forest Restoration Pact
sub: Less than 12% of Atlantic Forest remains. The Atlantic Forest Restoration Pact targets 15M ha by 2050. SOSMA and 30+ NGOs coordinate voluntary planting.

label: Colombia — Andean Forest Corridor Recovery
sub: Colombian government committed 1M ha restoration by 2030 under NYDF. Alto Magdalena watershed agroforestry supports 180,000 smallholders and Andean bear habitat.

label: Sundarbans — India/Bangladesh Mangrove Complex
sub: World's largest mangrove forest (10,000 km²). UNESCO World Heritage Site. Under threat from sea-level rise, cyclones, and shrimp farming. Joint restoration programs active.

label: Indonesia — Kalimantan Mangrove Rehabilitation
sub: World's largest national mangrove rehab program. 600,000 ha targeted by 2024 under BRGM. Indonesia holds 22% of global mangroves. Progress: ~400,000 ha active.

label: Guinea-Bissau — West African Mangrove Restoration
sub: West Africa holds 7% of global mangroves. Guinea-Bissau's community-managed mangroves cover ~220,000 ha. GIZ and IUCN support restoration of cleared aquaculture zones.

label: Kenya — Lamu Mangrove Recovery Zone
sub: Kenya's 61,000 ha of mangroves are legally protected since 2017. Lamu Archipelago is an active restoration site. MIKOKO PAMOJA is the world's first mangrove blue carbon project (ICROA-certified).

label: Mexico — Yucatán Mangrove Carbon Project
sub: Yucatán Peninsula holds the largest contiguous mangrove forest in the Americas. CONAFOR and WWF partner on restoration. Mangroves sequester 3–5x more carbon than terrestrial forests.

label: Singapore — City in a Garden Model
sub: Singapore's urban greening strategy covers 47% of land with vegetation. 4 million trees managed by NParks. National Parks Act mandates 0.8 ha green space per 1,000 residents.

label: Medellín — Urban Green Corridors
sub: 18 urban green corridors planted 2016–2022 reduced city temperatures by 2°C and increased urban tree canopy by 50%. Award-winning model for tropical cities. 880,000 trees planned city-wide.

label: London — Urban Rewilding & Tree Canopy
sub: London aims for 50% canopy cover in Outer London by 2050. Greater London Urban Forest project targets 135,000 new trees by 2025. First 'National Park City' designation in 2019.

label: Mumbai — Miyawaki Urban Micro-Forests
sub: Mumbai has deployed 60+ Miyawaki method micro-forests on previously degraded plots. Each 0.1–0.5 ha plot produces a dense, native-species forest patch in 10 years. BMC tree census counted 28 lakh trees citywide.

label: Borneo — Oil Palm / Forest Conversion Frontier
sub: Borneo has lost ~50% of its forest since 1950. Major conversion to oil palm continues in Kalimantan (Indonesia) and Sabah (Malaysia). RSPO certification covers ~20% of palm oil area.

label: Madagascar — Deforestation Crisis Zone
sub: Madagascar has lost ~90% of original forest. Annual deforestation ~100,000 ha. USAID and WCS-led restoration targets 40,000 ha. Critically endangered lemur habitat under threat.

label: Congo Basin — DRC Forest Recovery Zone
sub: Congo Basin holds 2nd largest tropical forest (3.3M km²). DRC signed COP26 declaration but deforestation accelerated 2022–2023. CAFI supports $500M protection program for 2030.

label: Sudan/Chad — Sahara Encroachment Zone
sub: Sahara has expanded southward ~48 km in recent decades. GGW corridor in Sudan and Chad faces acute conflict and climate pressure. Less than 5% of restoration target achieved in these zones.

label: Costa Rica — National Forest Recovery Model
sub: Forest cover increased from 21% (1987) to 60%+ (2022). Payment for Ecosystem Services (PES) program (FONAFIFO) credited with protecting 1M ha. A global model for reforestation policy.

label: Eastern Caribbean — REMAR Mangrove Restoration
sub: €5.5M REMAR project (2026–2031) restoring mangroves across Grenada, Saint Lucia, Saint Vincent & the Grenadines, Martinique, and Guadeloupe. OECS/AFD/FFEM partnership. Community-based, 3-pillar approach.

label: Odisha — Bhitarkanika Mangrove Recovery Model
sub: India's Odisha increased mangrove cover by 16.6% over a decade (222→259 km²). Bhitarkanika's 'fishbone channel' hydrological restoration technique regenerated 100 ha in 4 years. CAMPA-funded national model.
